name: cairn
description: Token-efficient agent harness with tool readiness for Codex, Claude Code, and Antigravity.

interface.shortDescription: Memory, planning, tool readiness, and two-gate verification harness for agent teams.
interface.longDescription: Cairn brings a LazyCodex-inspired flow to Codex, Claude Code, and Antigravity while reducing token use through repository-local MEMORY.md, PLAN.md, domain notes, tool readiness checks, module-task execution, and two-gate verification.
defaultPrompt: Use Cairn's repository-local PLAN.md and persisted goal state as the recovery spine; read root MEMORY.md first when it exists, and continue without repository memory when it does not.
For non-trivial work, load the phase skill (cairn-plan, cairn-work, or cairn-review), then restore the active plan and current-task references before acting.
After compaction, restart, handoff, or delegation, restore optional MEMORY.md → phase skill → active plan → current task references; do not rely on conversation recall.
A missing MEMORY.md never blocks work; fail closed only when active state, its skill, plan, or a required task reference is missing, unreadable, or inconsistent.
Missing, failed, skipped, stale, or placeholder evidence never completes work; advance UI state only after bound repository evidence passes.
Before external-state changes, run dry-run/check and bind the resulting evidence to the current task.
Answer side questions briefly, then resume the current task unless asked to pause, stop, or switch.
